L1.area: Data serialization and wire protocol handling layer

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the binary data encoding and decoding routines used for client-server protocol communication. A malicious server can send crafted responses that cause integer overflow due to the use of insufficiently sized signed integer types in the parsing logic, potentially leading to a crash or arbitrary code execution on the client.

L3.area: Data serialization and wire protocol handling layer
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the binary data encoding and decoding routines used for client-server protocol communication. A malicious server can send crafted responses that cause integer overflow due to the use of insufficiently sized signed integer types in the parsing logic, potentially leading to a crash or arbitrary code execution on the client.

ground_truth.reason: The variable-length integer read and write functions use Py_ssize_t (a signed, platform-dependent type) for accumulating results and shifts, allowing a malicious server to trigger an integer overflow that leads to a buffer overflow and potential arbitrary code execution on the client.
